intended_use: DeepTek CXR Analyzer V1.0 is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) software device that analyzes chest radiograph studies using machine learning techniques to identify, categorize, and highlight suspic
intended_use_full: DeepTek CXR Analyzer V1.0 is a computer-assisted detection (CADe) software device that analyzes chest radiograph studies using machine learning techniques to identify, categorize, and highlight suspicious ROIs in one of the following categories: Lungs, Pleura, Cardiac, and Hardware. The device is intended for use as a concurrent reading aid for radiologists. DeepTek CXR Analyzer V1.0 is indicated for adults and transitional adolescents (18 to <22 years old but treated like adults) only.
target_condition: Suspicious findings in chest radiographs (lung abnormalities, pleural findings, cardiac abnormalities, and hardware artifacts)
ground_truth: Majority opinion of 2 out of 3 U.S. board-certified radiologists (with 9, 11, and 25 years of experience); for localization, union of bounding boxes from all annotators identifying suspicious ROI for each category
other_metrics: wAFROC-FOM (weighted Alternative Free-Response ROC Figure of Merit): Standalone detection aggregate 0.920 [0.908-0.926]; Clinical aided reading aggregate 0.893 [0.871-0.914] vs unaided 0.821 [0.791-0.852], p<0.0001. Category-specific sensitivity: Lungs 0.903 [0.887-0.914], Pleura 0.924 [0.902-0.932], Cardiac 0.924 [0.890-0.952], Hardware 0.947 [0.936-0.955]. Category-specific specificity: Lungs 0.937 [0.927-0.948], Pleura 0.897 [0.879-0.911], Cardiac 0.930 [0.925-0.941], Hardware 0.947 [0.939-0.954]. Category-specific AUROC: Lungs 0.971 [0.968-0.976], Pleura 0.964 [0.954-0.970], Cardiac 0.978 [0.968-0.985], Hardware 0.980 [0.976-0.983].
